_description: Configure multiprocessing worker pool for DAG execution with CPU affinity

worker_pool._enabled_note: Set to true to enable worker pool multiprocessing. Default is false (single-process mode).
worker_pool._num_workers_note: Default is 4. Can also be set via application.properties: worker.pool.num_workers
worker_pool._worker_stabilization_note: Wait time after workers report ready before dispatching DAGs. Allows workers to complete module imports and setup.

communication._use_lmdb_note: Default false. The worker control plane (load/unload/status/heartbeat) always uses multiprocessing queues and is unaffected by this flag. This flag only pre-opens a per-worker LMDB env that the worker itself does not consume. DATA between DAGs on DIFFERENT worker processes flows through whatever transport the channels name: external brokers (kafka://, redis://, rabbitmq://, ...) are cross-process by nature and work regardless; lmdb://channel endpoints also work regardless (they manage their own env from the URI). With this flag false, in-memory (mem://) channels are WORKER-LOCAL - they only connect DAGs co-located in the same worker process, not across workers.
